new_targets.why: Official Solana Foundation CLI for agentic payments (x402, MPP, AP2). 1.7k★, very active (updated yesterday), and maintained by the foundation itself — a merged PR here carries institutional credibility. Multi-protocol coverage means one integration demonstrates Bolyra across x402, MPP, and AP2 simultaneously.
new_targets.gap: CLI orchestrates payment flows for autonomous agents with no identity check on the calling agent. Any process with the right wallet key can spend — no authorization scope enforcement. Bolyra adds: verify agent credential + permission bits (FINANCIAL_SMALL/MEDIUM/UNLIMITED) before payment dispatch, with on-chain nullifier for key rotation.
new_targets.suggested_pr: docs/example: add Bolyra agent credential check before x402/MPP/AP2 payment dispatch
new_targets.contact_hint: Rust repo — target the docs/ or examples/ directory. Check CONTRIBUTING.md for DCO/CLA requirements.

new_targets.why: 1k★ trust layer for agent-to-agent commerce — ERC-7710 delegated permissions, x402 payments, escrow, and dispute resolution as a Claude Code plugin. Updated today, very active. Their own framing ('trust layer') overlaps perfectly with Bolyra's value prop, making this a high-resonance pitch.
new_targets.gap: Handles on-chain delegation via ERC-7710 but lacks ZKP-based identity: there is no proof that the agent initiating a mandate actually holds the credentials it claims, and no nullifier to prevent replay of a mandate. Bolyra adds the ZKP identity layer that makes their delegation claims cryptographically binding rather than policy-only.
new_targets.suggested_pr: feat/example: add Bolyra ZKP agent identity check as prerequisite for internet-court mandate submission
new_targets.contact_hint: Very active repo, updated today. TypeScript, Apache-2.0 topics. Open issues present — check for any auth/identity issues to reference in PR description.

new_targets.why: 506★ open-source autonomous trading agent built on Claude, covering 1000+ markets (Polymarket, Kalshi, Binance, Hyperliquid, Solana DEXs, 5 EVM chains) with x402 machine-to-machine payments. The stakes are real: if this agent is compromised or misconfigured, it spends real money on real markets. This is the canonical use case for FINANCIAL_UNLIMITED permission enforcement.
new_targets.gap: Agent executes trades and M2M payments with no authorization credential on the calling context. An operator deploying multiple CloddsBot instances cannot enforce which instance is authorized to trade which markets or up to what spend limit. Bolyra's permission bits (FINANCIAL_SMALL/MEDIUM/UNLIMITED) map directly to the bot's position-size tiers.
new_targets.suggested_pr: docs/example: add Bolyra agent credential check before trade execution and x402 payment dispatch

new_targets.why: 486★ privacy layer for AI agents on Solana — stealth addresses + x402. Updated today. They solve payment privacy (who receives money); Bolyra solves agent authorization (who is allowed to spend). These are orthogonal and compose naturally: first prove the agent is authorized (Bolyra), then make the payment unlinkable (ProwlFi).
new_targets.gap: Agent proves nothing about its authorization before generating a stealth payment — any agent with a key can spend. The combination 'Bolyra credential check → ProwlFi stealth dispatch' creates a complete privacy + authorization stack. A PR adding a Bolyra pre-flight check before stealth dispatch would be the first example of this composed pattern.
new_targets.suggested_pr: docs/example: add Bolyra authorization check as pre-flight before ProwlFi stealth payment dispatch
new_targets.contact_hint: Repo is small (2 open issues). Maintainer likely solo — keep the PR minimal (one example file, no new dependencies).

new_targets.why: 260★ drop-in OpenAI Python client with transparent x402 payment support. Any Python agent using the OpenAI API can bolt on x402 payments with no code changes. Zero open issues suggests clean, maintained codebase. Adding a Bolyra pre-flight here means every x402-enabled OpenAI call also verifies agent identity — one small example, high distribution.
new_targets.gap: Client wraps OpenAI API with x402 payment but has no concept of which agent is authorized to use the client or what spend limit applies. Any process that holds the API key can issue unlimited paid calls. Bolyra adds: require a valid AgentPolicy credential with FINANCIAL_SMALL/MEDIUM permission before the client will execute a paid call.
new_targets.suggested_pr: docs/example: add Bolyra agent credential gate before x402-enabled OpenAI API calls

follow_ups_drafted.comment: Hey! Just following up on this docs PR — it's been almost a month with no activity, which I completely understand for an async open-source project. I'd like to close this out rather than leave noise in your issue tracker. If any part of the agent identity / per-tool authorization angle is interesting for a future contribution, I'm happy to reopen or reformat. Otherwise I'll close this in a few days. Thanks for maintaining open-stocks-mcp!

Hi! Following up on this agent identity guide — it's been about four weeks. I understand Stripe docs likely have an internal review process, so no rush. If a different format works better (a code example, an SDK integration snippet, or a separate example repo that you could link from your own docs), I'm happy to pivot completely. And if this PR isn't the right vehicle, just say the word and I'll close it without issue. Thanks for building great payment infrastructure!

Hey! Following up on this agent authorization guide — nearly a month with no activity. For brokerage APIs specifically, the idea of verifying an AI agent's credential before it places orders seems worth having in the docs somewhere, but I don't want to leave stale PRs open. If this isn't the right format or doesn't fit your roadmap, I'll close it and happily open a narrower example instead. Just let me know!

Hey! Following up on the agent authorization guide after almost a month. Happy to close this if it's not fitting — I don't want to leave noise in your tracker. If the idea of a ZKP-based agent credential check before order/inventory mutations is interesting, I could reformat as a much shorter README snippet instead. Either way, thanks for building a clean Shopify MCP!
